description: The ancient architectural complex from the Ming and Qing Dynasties, now the Confucian Temple branch of the Zhengzhou Museum, showcasing ancient educational culture.
A well-known comprehensive shopping center in Zhengzhou, offering a full range of brands and integrating shopping and dining.
The central scenic lake of the CBD in Zhengdong New District, famous for its nighttime light shows.
A professional museum fully showcasing the history and culture of Yellow River management.
An ecological wetland park in Zhengdong New District, with beautiful surroundings, ideal for leisure walks and sightseeing.
A large cultural theme park on the southern bank of the Yellow River, offering elevated views of the river and an immersive experience of Yellow River culture.
A landmark square in downtown Zhengzhou, with the Erqi Memorial Tower as a symbolic architectural landmark of the city.
One of China's first national first-class museums, featuring a collection of Central Plains cultural treasures and rich exhibits.
A modernly designed comprehensive museum with cultural exhibitions themed around the local history and culture of Zhengzhou.
The ruins of the Shang Dynasty capital from 3,600 years ago, featuring the earliest surviving ancient city walls in China.
A significant historical site of the Yellow River, where the 1938 Huayuankou breach event occurred, blending historical culture with natural scenery.
A comprehensive museum showcasing the historical and cultural development of Zhengzhou.
A historic Taoist temple in Zhengzhou with traditional architectural styles and a thriving incense-burning tradition.
A prime scenic area of Shaoshi Mountain in Songshan, renowned for its steep peaks and hanging plank roads.

attraction_name: Zhengzhou Shangdu National Archaeological Site Park
description: The ruins of the Shang Dynasty capital, a historical landmark in the city center, showcasing the civilization of over 3,500 years ago.
A landmark building in Zhengzhou, commemorating the historic strike of workers on the Beijing-Hankou Railway, particularly striking at night.
A landmark building in the Zhengzhou CBD, nicknamed 'Big Corn' for its corn-shaped appearance, with stunning nighttime lighting effects.
A Yellow River culture-themed park, featuring the iconic giant statues of the Yan and Huang Emperors.
